message.content:
Explore the test infrastructure in this project:
1. Find all test files (*.test.ts, *.spec.ts)
2. Check the test configuration (vitest.config.ts or similar)
3. Look at package.json scripts for test commands
4. Identify what types of tests exist (unit, integration, e2e)
5. Check if there are any test utilities or helpers

Report the complete test structure and available test commands.

content.text: I'll explore the test infrastructure in this project systematically. Let me start by finding test files, configuration, and understanding the test setup.

content.content:
1→{
     2→  "name": "belt",
     3→  "version": "0.1.0",
     4→  "private": true,
     5→  "description": "TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers",
     6→  "repository": {
     7→    "type": "git",
     8→    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt"
     9→  },
    10→  "author": "@antoniopresto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
    11→  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.14.2",
    12→  "engines": {
    13→    "node": ">=20.0.0"
    14→  },
    15→  "scripts": {
    16→    "build": "turbo run build",
    17→    "dev": "turbo run dev",
    18→    "lint": "biome check .",
    19→    "lint:fix": "biome check --write .",
    20→    "format": "biome format --write .",
    21→    "test": "turbo run test",
    22→    "typecheck": "turbo run typecheck",
    23→    "clean": "turbo run clean && rm -rf node_modules",
    24→    "prepare": "husky"
    25→  },
    26→  "devDependencies": {
    27→    "@biomejs/biome": "^2.3.11",
    28→    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    29→    "husky": "9.1.7",
    30→    "turbo": "^2.7.2",
    31→    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
    32→  }
    33→}
    34→

file.content:
{
  "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/tsconfig",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "lib": ["ES2022"],
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "strict": true,
    "strictNullChecks": true,
    "strictFunctionTypes": true,
    "strictBindCallApply": true,
    "strictPropertyInitialization": true,
    "noImplicitAny": true,
    "noImplicitThis": true,
    "noImplicitReturns": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true
  },
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}

content.text: Let me check for test utilities or helpers and explore the entire test structure:
Perfect. Let me check the project structure and look for any existing vitest configuration in node_modules or elsewhere:

input.description: Check vitest CLI help and version
List source files to understand project structure

content.content:
vitest/4.0.16

Usage:
  $ vitest [...filters]

Commands:
  run [...filters]      
  related [...filters]  
  watch [...filters]    
  dev [...filters]      
  bench [...filters]    
  init <project>        
  list [...filters]     
  [...filters]          

For more info, run any command with the `--help` flag:
  $ vitest run --help
  $ vitest related --help
  $ vitest watch --help
  $ vitest dev --help
  $ vitest bench --help
  $ vitest init --help
  $ vitest list --help
  $ vitest --help
  $ vitest --help --expand-help

Options:
  -v, --version                                         Display version number 
  -r, --root <path>                                     Root path 
  -c, --config <path>                                   Path to config file 
  -u, --update                                          Update snapshot 
  -w, --watch                                           Enable watch mode 
  -t, --testNamePattern <pattern>                       Run tests with full names matching the specified regexp pattern 
  --dir <path>                                          Base directory to scan for the test files 
  --ui                                                  Enable UI 
  --open                                                Open UI automatically (default: !process.env.CI) 
  --api [port]                                          Specify server port. Note if the port is already being used, Vite will automatically try the next available port so this may not be the actual port the server ends up listening on. If true will be set to 51204. Use '--help --api' for more info. 
  --silent [value]                                      Silent console output from tests. Use 'passed-only' to see logs from failing tests only. 
  --hideSkippedTests                                    Hide logs for skipped tests 
  --reporter <name>                                     Specify reporters (default, blob, verbose, dot, json, tap, tap-flat, junit, tree, hanging-process, github-actions) 
  --outputFile <filename/-s>                            Write test results to a file when supporter reporter is also specified, use cac's dot notation for individual outputs of multiple reporters (example: --outputFile.tap=./tap.txt) 
  --coverage                                            Enable coverage report. Use '--help --coverage' for more info. 
  --mode <name>                                         Override Vite mode (default: test or benchmark) 
  --isolate                                             Run every test file in isolation. To disable isolation, use --no-isolate (default: true) 
  --globals                                             Inject apis globally 
  --dom                                                 Mock browser API with happy-dom 
  --browser <name>                                      Run tests in the browser. Equivalent to --browser.enabled (default: false). Use '--help --browser' for more info. 
  --pool <pool>                                         Specify pool, if not running in the browser (default: forks) 
  --execArgv <option>                                   Pass additional arguments to node process when spawning worker_threads or child_process. 
  --vmMemoryLimit <limit>                               Memory limit for VM pools. If you see memory leaks, try to tinker this value.

Note if the port is already being used, Vite will automatically try the next available port so this may not be the actual port the server ends up listening on. If true will be set to 51204. Use '--help --api' for more info.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive report of the test infrastructure.

## Estrutura de Testes Completa do Projeto Belt

### 1. Arquivos de Teste Encontrados

**Localização única de testes:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/schema/lib/__tests__/type.spec.ts` (7.3 KB)

**Padrão utilizado:** `*.spec.ts` (arquivo de especificação unitária)

Apenas 1 arquivo de teste existe no projeto atualmente, cobrindo apenas o módulo de validação de tipos.

---

### 2. Configuração de Testes (vitest.config)

**Status:** Não há arquivo `vitest.config.ts` explícito

O projeto utiliza a configuração padrão do Vitest 4.0.16, sem customizações visíveis. A configuração é herdada das convenções padrão:
- Procura por testes em padrões: `**/*.spec.ts`, `**/*.test.ts`, `**/__tests__/**/*`
- Globals injetadas por padrão

---

### 3. Comandos de Teste Disponíveis

**Root (`/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/package.json`):**
```bash
pnpm test              # Executa vitest via turbo
pnpm typecheck        # Verifica tipos
```

**Package (@belt/tools `/packages/tools/package.json`):**
```bash
pnpm test              # vitest (execução única)
pnpm test:watch       # vitest --watch (modo observação)
pnpm coverage         # vitest --coverage (relatório de cobertura)
```

---

### 4. Tipos de Testes Identificados

**Tipo identificado: Unit Tests**
- Testes de validação de tipos com TypeBox
- Validação de primitivos (string, number, boolean, null, undefined)
- Validação de tipos complexos (objects, arrays, unions)
- Testes de propriedades opcionais
- Testes de mensagens de erro
- Testes de métodos `check()`, `parse()`, `errors()`, `decode()`

**Cobertura atual:**
- Schema validation library (`schema/lib/type.ts`)
- Type inference (`TypeBoxInfer`)
- Custom date type validation

**Tipos NÃO presentes:**
- Integration tests
- E2E tests
- Tests para state-machine
- Tests para helpers

---

### 5. Estrutura de Testes Detalhada

```
packages/tools/
├── src/
│   ├── helpers/              (17 módulos - SEM TESTES)
│   │   ├── compose.ts
```

---

### 6. Configuração TypeScript para Testes

**tsconfig.json (root):**
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "strict": true,
    "noImplicitAny": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true
  },
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

**tsconfig.json (@belt/tools):**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "declaration": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler"
  },
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", "**/*.test.ts", "**/*.spec.ts"]
}
```

**Nota:** Tests são excluídos da compilação TypeScript final (apenas `.ts` fonte compilado).

---

### 7. Dependências de Teste

**Vitest 4.0.16** é a única ferramenta de teste declarada:
```json
{
  "devDependencies": {
    "vitest": "^4.0.16"
  }
}
```

**Frameworks de teste NÃO configurados:**
- Jest
- Mocha
- Chai
- Testing Library

---

### 8. Exemplo de Estrutura de Teste

O arquivo `type.spec.ts` demonstra a estrutura completa:

```typescript
import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
import { createBaseType, createDateType } from '../type';

describe('schema/lib/type', () => {
  describe('createBaseType', () => {
    describe('with primitive types', () => {
      it('should validate strings', () => {
        const stringType = createBaseType(Type.String());
        expect(stringType.check('hello')).toBe(true);
        expect(() => stringType.parse(123)).toThrow();
      });
      // ... mais testes
    });
  });
});
```

**Métodos testados:**
- `.check()` - validação booleana
- `.parse()` - parsing com exceção
- `.errors()` - array de mensagens de erro
- `.decode()` - desserialização

---

### 9. Build & Test Workflow

**Git Hooks (Husky):**
```
pre-commit:  pnpm lint
pre-push:    pnpm build && pnpm typecheck  (sem tests!)
commit-msg:  validação de conventional commits
```

**Observação crítica:** `pre-push` NÃO executa `pnpm test`. Isso significa testes podem falhar em produção sem bloquear pushes.

---

### 10. Resumo de Gaps na Cobertura de Testes

| Módulo | Status | Gap |
|--------|--------|-----|
| schema/lib/type.ts | ✓ Testado | - |
| schema/helpers.ts | ✗ | Funções de schema auxiliares |
| schema/infer-type.ts | ✗ | Type inference logic |
| schema/schema.ts | ✗ | Schema builder core |
| state-machine/* | ✗ | 11 arquivos sem testes |
| helpers/* | ✗ | 17 arquivos sem testes |
| **TOTAL** | **1 arquivo** | **40+ arquivos sem cobertura** |

---

### Conclusão

O projeto está em **fase inicial de test coverage**. Apenas a camada de validação de tipos base possui testes unitários. Não há testes de integração, E2E, ou cobertura para os principais módulos de state-machine e helpers. 

**Recomendação:** Implementar `vitest.config.ts` explícito, expandir cobertura de testes, e adicionar execução de testes ao hook `pre-push`.
